author: @Ian.Gostling
text: Well said Rob, fear is used as a control method in society and how many people like to comply!

author: @charlesmccracken5936
text: I am definitely with you. However, I wish I would’ve listened to other people when I stuck my finger in that light socket. Lol.

author: @timgchannel3328
text: I was hit by a flying chuck key. Harry Nelson threw it at me in shop class. I had just told him not to leave the chuck key in the lathe.

author: @VeniceInventors
text: I like the chuck example, "know the specific circumstances" is so easy to overlook.

author: @OMIF1
text: Just yesterday my wife read me a little post about someone leaving two small, old dogs tied to a fence. My first thought was: How could anyone do anything so horrible, animal abuse just infuriates me. Then I thought, wait, maybe this was the hardest thing this person ever did, abandoning his/her beloved pets because, perhaps, they could no longer care for them. I was immediately taken by how quick one can jump to potentially wrong conclusions and think the worst of someone you know nothing about. Merry Christmas.

author: @richardclark5112
text: I like that you are in good control of the channel behaviour . Stops things getting out of hand.

author: @peterbarrett5829
text: I hope to never come across a flying Chucky! Merry Christmas!

author: @offgridwanabe
text: Yes while in school in Canada it was drilled into us not to leave the key in the chuck perhaps we have been trained to have fear and the problem is deeper than what we know to be safe. He claimed the hole in the block wall was left by a former student. I did see the mill come out of the floor bolts one day lol many years ago as I am older the Robert....

author: @zenile7087
text:
Hi from France,

Manu thanks for these topics, an action which is far more dangerous is using sand paper / abrasive media HELD BY HANDS.

Within milliseconds our hands can be snapped; by sudden adherence made by buildup of heat, dust.

Lots of companies shall display by law interdiction of such action, a private company is proposing a dedicated holder in order to mitigate.

The speed can be seen, never the torque.

But you are right, life is risky to death, we should act wisely.

Best regards, Rob and all tinkerers !

author: @pedjamilosavljevic6235
text: I grew up in 1970's. You may not believe this , but we all drove our push bikes (and mopeds) without helmets , let alone knee and elbow pads and gloves. How did we survived ? I think , that all that you've talked about is connected with age , circumstances of growing up and place (country) where people live, or grew up. I had close fly by (like few centimeters near my head) of chuck key , from a big , old lathe chuck. Only once. Real life experience and virtual/TV experience aren't the same , so , because people mix it , we live in world as it currently is.

author: @bryanst.martin7134
text: It isn't uncommon for people that know practically nothing to speak as if they do. It makes them feel better, but not actually help. I had a supervisor at a truck modification facility, sort of like that. I had the dash panel off and had to hold it while using a jig saw to cut out the switch perimeter to later mount a laser cut Ally panel. Dangerous? Sure, many things makers do can be. His solution? "Hey Bryan!!! Be careful!". This is the kind of person that you cringe when you see him with a screw gun in his hand. I was on top of one of the trucks working on a strobe internals, I tagged out the breaker, and while I was checking connections inside he turns the breaker on. 1500 volts! Fortunately I was a life long auto mech and high voltage didn't knock me over as another might be. Some people belong behind a desk.

author: @ibme8359
text: The "chuck key", I had to go "fishing' to find mine on occasion for the one for my drill press (Yes I'm one of those who am concerned about "flying chuck keys", never got hit but had a "fly by or two" and have found a "usual" place to store it and try to be mindful when I remove it to that location! Well that's my solution, just thought I'd "chime in" on that one!!! Have a Merry Christmas and a Happy New Year!!! I for one am looking forward to more of your (EVERYTHING)!!!

author: @scotttovey
text:
Matthias Wandel just recently received the same or similar lathe and in response to the dangers of leaving the key in the chuck he disabled the safety locks and tested to see how far the key would fly.
Here's a link to his video.

It turns out that the dangers of leaving the chuck key in the chuck, is in full sized lathes that do not have the safety features.

How far will the chuck key fly?
https://youtu.be/n-YAWpAmioQ?feature=shared

author: @AndreaDingbatt
text:
I hope that my daft sense of humour hasn't been offending anyone,,,I hope that anything is taken in good humour and if I accidentally write anything out of order, I don't mind if I am told, that I have been!!
Thank you Robert, I hope that you and your family have a wonderful Xmas and a happy New Year !! 🎉
